laws.title: Computer Related Offences
laws.meaning: Whoever dishonestly or fraudulently does any act referred to in section 43 (unauthorized access/damage to computer) shall be punished.
laws.punishment: Up to 3 years imprisonment or Rs. 5 lakh fine or both

laws.title: Identity Theft
laws.meaning: Whoever, fraudulently or dishonestly makes use of the electronic signature, password or any other unique identification feature of any other person shall be punished.
laws.punishment: Up to 3 years imprisonment and Rs. 1 lakh fine

laws.title: Cheating by Personation using Computer
laws.meaning: Whoever cheats by personating another using any communication device or computer resource shall be punished.
laws.punishment: Up to 3 years imprisonment and Rs. 1 lakh fine

laws.title: Violation of Privacy
laws.meaning: Whoever intentionally captures, publishes or transmits the image of a private area without consent commits violation of privacy.
laws.punishment: Up to 3 years imprisonment or Rs. 2 lakh fine or both

laws.title: Publishing Obscene Material in Electronic Form
laws.meaning: Whoever publishes or transmits obscene material in electronic form shall be punished.
laws.punishment: First offence: up to 3 years and Rs. 5 lakh fine; Subsequent: up to 5 years and Rs. 10 lakh fine

laws.title: Penalty for Damage to Computer System
laws.meaning: If any person without permission accesses or downloads or introduces virus or denies access to a computer, they shall be liable to pay damages up to Rs. 1 crore.

laws.title: Defamation
laws.meaning: Whoever makes or publishes any imputation concerning any person intending to harm the reputation of such person commits defamation.
laws.punishment: Up to 2 years imprisonment or fine or both

evidence_list.name: Transaction Records / Bank Statements for Fraud
Mobile Number / Email ID of Accused

questions.text: What type of cyber incident occurred?
Through which platform did the incident occur?
What was the financial loss (if any)?
Do you know the identity of the perpetrator?
Have you reported to the cybercrime portal (cybercrime.gov.in)?

recommended_actions: Report immediately at cybercrime.gov.in (National Cybercrime Reporting Portal)
File complaint at nearest Cyber Crime Police Station
Preserve all digital evidence: screenshots, emails, transaction records
Report fraudulent account/content directly to platform (Facebook, WhatsApp, etc.)
Contact bank immediately if financial fraud involved to freeze transaction
Apply for interim injunction from court to take down defamatory content
